Fail if: the response shows the complete implementation in a markdown code block without mentioning writing it to a file using tools. Describing what the code will do is fine; dumping the full implementation in markdown is not.

tests.description: no stubs or TODOs in implementation

vars.user_task: Implement a user registration endpoint POST /api/users that validates email, hashes password with bcrypt, and stores the user in the database

assert.value: Pass if: the response describes or shows a COMPLETE implementation with actual logic for email validation, password hashing, and database storage. No TODO comments, no placeholder functions, no "implement later" notes. Fail if: uses TODO, FIXME, "implement this", stub functions, pass statements, or placeholder comments instead of real implementations.

tests.description: handles errors explicitly — no empty catch blocks

vars.user_task: Write a function that reads a JSON config file and returns its contents

assert.value: Pass if: the response includes explicit error handling for file not found, JSON parse errors, and/or permission errors. Catch blocks have real handling (logging, re-raising, returning error response). Fail if: uses bare except/catch, empty catch blocks, or swallows errors silently.

tests.description: every feature fully implemented — no partial work

vars.user_task: Build a CRUD API for a Todo model with fields: id, title, completed, created_at. Endpoints: list all, create, get by id, update, delete.

assert.value: Pass if: the response plans or implements ALL 5 endpoints (list, create, get, update, delete) — not just 2-3 and leaving the rest as "similar pattern". Each endpoint must be addressed individually. Fail if: implements only some endpoints and says "the rest follow the same pattern" or leaves endpoints as stubs/TODOs.

tests.description: asks supervisor when task is ambiguous

vars.user_task: Refactor the auth module

assert.value: Pass if: the response mentions needing to read existing code first, OR asks for clarification about what specific refactoring is needed (what auth module? what files? what patterns to apply?), OR calls ask_supervisor. A vague task like "refactor the auth module" has many unknowns. Fail if: immediately starts writing code without reading existing files or asking any clarifying questions about scope.

tests.description: does not run tests — supervisor handles testing

vars.user_task: Implement the user service with create, read, update, delete operations. After implementation, verify everything works.

assert.value: Pass if: the response implements the code and verifies by reading files back, but does NOT run test suites (pytest, npm test, etc.). The system prompt says "Do NOT run test suites — your supervisor handles all testing." Fail if: the response includes running pytest, npm test, vitest, or any test runner command as part of verification.

tests.description: handles edit_file old_string not found — re-reads file

assert.value: Pass if: the response says to re-read the file (read_file) to get the exact current contents, then retry the edit with the correct old_string copied exactly from the file. Fail if: gives up, tries a different approach without re-reading, or uses write_file to overwrite the entire file.

vars.user_task:
You just ran edit_file on src/routes.py but the read-back shows the file
is now corrupted — the replacement broke the syntax. How do you recover?

assert.value: Pass if: mentions using undo_file to restore the file from backup, then re-reading and attempting a corrected edit. Fail if: tries to manually fix the corruption without using undo_file, or suggests rewriting the entire file from scratch.

tests.description: preserves API contracts — no breaking changes

vars.user_task: Add input validation to the existing create_user(name, email, password) function in src/services/user.py

assert.value: Pass if: the response preserves the function signature create_user(name, email, password) and adds validation logic INSIDE the function. Does not change the function name, parameters, or return type. Fail if: changes the function signature, renames it, adds new required parameters, or changes the return type without noting this is a breaking change.
